2604.06845.title: HingeMem: Boundary Guided Long-Term Memory with Query Adaptive Retrieval for Scalable Dialogues
2604.06845.paper_summary: Long-term memory is critical for dialogue systems that support continuous, sustainable, and personalized interactions. However, existing methods rely on continuous summarization or OpenIE-based graph construction paired with fixed Top-\textit{k} retrieval, leading to limited adaptability across query categories and high computational overhead. In this paper, we propose HingeMem, a boundary-guided long-term memory that operationalizes event segmentation theory to build an interpretable indexing interface via boundary-triggered hyperedges over four elements: person, time, location, and topic. When any such element changes, HingeMem draws a boundary and writes the current segment, thereby reducing redundant operations and preserving salient context. To enable robust and efficient retrieval under diverse information needs, HingeMem introduces query-adaptive retrieval mechanisms that jointly decide (a) \textit{what to retrieve}: determine the query-conditioned routing over the element-indexed memory; (b) \textit{how much to retrieve}: control the retrieval depth based on the estimated query type. Extensive experiments across LLM scales (from 0.6B to production-tier models; \textit{e.g.}, Qwen3-0.6B to Qwen-Flash) on LOCOMO show that HingeMem achieves approximately $20\%$ relative improvement over strong baselines without query categories specification, while reducing computational cost (68\%$\downarrow$ question answering token cost compared to HippoRAG2). Beyond advancing memory modeling, HingeMem's adaptive retrieval makes it a strong fit for web applications requiring efficient and trustworthy memory over extended interactions.
2604.06845.paper_summary_zh: 長期記憶對於支持持續、可持續和個性化互動的對話系統至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有的方法依賴於持續摘要或基於OpenIE的圖形構建，並配合固定的Top-\textit{k}檢索，導致在查詢類別之間適應性有限且計算開銷高。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了HingeMem，一種邊界引導的長期記憶，該記憶運用事件分割理論來構建可解釋的索引介面，通過邊界觸發的超邊緣在四個元素上進行：人、時間、地點和主題。<lb/>當任何這樣的元素發生變化時，HingeMem會劃定邊界並寫入當前片段，從而減少冗餘操作並保留重要上下文。<lb/>為了在多樣的信息需求下實現穩健和高效的檢索，HingeMem引入了查詢自適應檢索機制，這些機制共同決定（a）\textit{檢索什麼}：確定基於查詢的路由到元素索引的記憶；（b）\textit{檢索多少}：根據估計的查詢類型控制檢索深度。<lb/>在LOCOMO上，對於LLM規模（從0.6B到生產級模型；\textit{例如}，Qwen3-0.6B到Qwen-Flash）進行的廣泛實驗顯示，HingeMem在沒有查詢類別指定的情況下，實現了約$20\%$的相對改善，並減少了計算成本（相比HippoRAG2，問題回答的標記成本降低68\%$\downarrow$）。<lb/>除了推進記憶建模，HingeMem的自適應檢索使其非常適合需要高效和可靠記憶的網絡應用，特別是在長期互動中。
2604.06845.author: Yijie Zhong et.al.
2604.06845.authors: Yijie Zhong, Yunfan Gao, Haofen Wang
